ddls:
CREATE TABLE "machinery" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "machine_type" TEXT /* Type of the machine */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the machine was installed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the machinery table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record of sensor data */,
  "timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp for when the data was recorded */,
  "ir_sensor" REAL /* Reading from the infrared sensor */,
  "x_axis" REAL /* Position on the X-axis */,
  "y_axis" REAL /* Position on the Y-axis */,
  "z_axis" REAL /* Position on the Z-axis */,
  "velocity" REAL /* Velocity measured from the sensor */,
  "acc_1d" REAL /* One-dimensional acceleration measured from the sensor */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the sensor table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the sensor table */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., high, low, range) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Value of the threshold */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_history" (
  "alert_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert history record */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the alerts table */,
  "timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp for when the alert was triggered */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the sensor_data table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role (e.g., admin, engineer, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the machinery table */,
  "read_access" INTEGER /* Read access flag */,
  "write_access" INTEGER /* Write access flag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)
